feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Unable to configure US Benefits in SuccessFactors Sales demo instance.
entry.content:
Hi All

We have already EC Core setup done in the SuccessFactors Sales Demo instance. We are trying to run the upgrade for US Benefits Localizations in the upgrade center.

There are prerequisites for US Benefits Localization which are EC US localization and EC core setup. Since EC core setup was already done, I am not sure why it is showing as pending prerequisites on the upgrade page. I ran the EC Core again and below is the message for the issue.


We got the below error message:

-----------------------------------------------

[Best Practices Configurations Employee Central Core (XX) Begins, The value of Picklist migration is true. Will continue the upgrade with MDF Picklists, -------Preparing for Upgrade------ , Provisioning access granted to the user, ------Performing Upgrade------- , 'Employee Central V2' is activated in Provisioning. Now the upgrade can continue,
BPMsg: There are existing foundation objects: EVENT_REASON. Upgrade cannot continue, Upgrade Failed; All changes will rollback.
For further help with this error, please raise a ticket in the component LOD-SF-SBP.]

----------------------------------------

Please share your experiences and thoughts on the next steps to resolve.

entry.title: Edit SAP Success Factors Timeout Landing Page
entry.content:
When users Timeout they are presented with a page asking them to follow a link to log back in. I would like to edit this page and i cannot find an area in the admin centre where this is possible.

 

Does anybody know where or how to edit the landing page for when a user is timed out of the system?

entry.title: City for country Colombia is not replicated.
entry.content:
Has anyone also had the problem with the replication of the city in Colombia?

The mapping for the address replication is created. The City is also displayed in the interface, but does not moved to P0006-ORT01 in SAP ERP.

Thank you for your responce.

entry.title: Email in SF
entry.content:
Is it possible to set different email addresses, but with the same domain, for each module, from which modules does SF send notification?

For example:

- for RCM: recruitment@example.com

- for ONB: onbarding@example.com

- for EC: employment@example.com

entry.title: Mapping Salary from REC Offer Template to EC Compensation Info
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

Our customer requires to map the Salary from Offer Template to the Compensation Information Pay Components. 

Field in Offer Template: Salary (general string)

Pay Components in Comp Info: BI-weekly or Hourly 

There are two salary types:

  • Bi-weekly - the annualized amount is entered in the Offer Template in the field "Salary" (string)
  • Hourly - the hourly amount will be entered in the Offer Template in the field "Salary" (string)

The idea is to map them in a way that one of these two values, depending on the Employee Subgroup, is mapped to either the Pay Component or Pay Component Group. 

Does anyone know if this is possible to do via Recruit to Hire template or any custom solution?

Thank you in advance. 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile  SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting

entry.title: Time sheet and overtime automatic submission and approval daily
entry.content:
Hello all, 

 

I have this issue that salary has to be calculated each 1st of month and overtime is considered. We are not using time tracking. When monday is on 30 lets say and month has 31 days, for overtime that is done on 31 st we need to wait next monday to be approved as this is how it is time sheet process  without time tracking. So the salary that needs to be calculated on1st wont have those OT hours that are done on 30 and on 31 st. I was thinking to delete workflow and only manager to be notified and this to go to automatic approval and submission... is that possible?

entry.title: SF mobile app download and usage monitoring
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Is there a way to report on SF mobile app download and usage?

I know that in manage mobile user, we can export all mobile users, however, is there a way to know who is actively using the mobile application (usage)?

Many thanks!

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Optimizing Contingent Worker Management in SAP SuccessFactors: Preventing Licensing and Data Pitfall
entry.content:
Introduction

Contingent workers have become a core part of today’s flexible workforce. SAP SuccessFactors offers dedicated functionality to manage contingent workers separately from full-time employees (FTEs). However, incorrect configuration can lead to license mismanagement, reporting inaccuracies, and non-compliance. In this blog, I share a real-world scenario where contingent workers were mistakenly configured as FTEs and the step-by-step process I followed to resolve it.

 

The Problem: Contingent Workers Consuming FTE Licenses

At a client site, contingent workers were mistakenly onboarded using hiring actions designed for FTEs—such as "Hire - New Hire" and "Hire - Replacement." This led to a cascade of issues:

 

  • Consumed FTE licenses unintentionally
  • Were counted incorrectly in organizational reporting
  • Showed active status in the system even after separation

The root cause was a misunderstanding of how contingent worker events differ from FTE actions in SAP SuccessFactors.

 

Business Impact

This configuration error caused ripple effects across multiple business functions:

  • Licensing Cost Overruns: Unnecessary usage of paid FTE licenses
  • Compliance Risks: Active records without valid contracts or dates
  • Data Integrity Issues: Inaccurate workforce analytics and planning reports

 

Resolution Strategy: How We Fixed It Step-by-Step

Here’s the remediation process I followed, based on SAP best practices:

          Step 1: Extract Existing Job Information

  • Navigate to Reporting > All Reports
  • Run and download the Job Info export to identify affected records

     

    Step 2: Prepare Corrected Job History Template

    • Go to Import Employee Data
    • Download the Job Information Template
    • Populate the correct event reasons (e.g., "Start CWK" and proper termination reasons)

  • Ensure all actions are in sequential order

    Step 3: Re-import the Job Information

    • Import the corrected template
    • Use Full Purge to replace faulty job Information entries
    • Validate that contingent workers now reflect correct statuses and do not consume FTE licenses
    • This fix was tested and confirmed in a sandbox before applying to production.

    In line with best practices, this is the recommended approach to resolve the existing contingent worker data and accurately update their termination status.

     

    Best Practice: Hiring a Contingent Worker the Right Way

    To prevent recurrence, we established a proper process aligned with SAP best practices:

    Step 1: Initiate from Contingent Worker Template

    • Navigate to Add Contingent Worker

           Step 2: Fill Personal & Assignment Information

  • Select event reason Start CWK

 

  • Enter Contract End Date in both Assignment Info and Work Order section

    Step 3: Submit

    • Review the details and click Submit
    • The system will now correctly identify this worker as contingent, not consuming FTE license.

 

Key Takeaways

  • Always align event reasons with worker types (FTE vs. CWK)
  • Align with HR, IT, and system integrators to maintain consistent data entry standards.
  • Schedule routine audits to catch anomalies early before they escalate

Conclusion & Call to Action

A seemingly small configuration gap can result in real business cost and risk. Thankfully, SAP SuccessFactors provides the tools needed—what’s critical is following the right methodology. I hope this experience helps your organization avoid these pitfalls and confidently manage contingent workers the right way.

Have you dealt with similar issues in your SuccessFactors setup? Share your experiences or questions in the comments—let’s learn from each other!

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: Does Event Reasons/Actions supports Manual Document Generation?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I understand that Using Event Reasons/Actions in business rules is not feasible/supported for AUTOMATIC Document Generation as per the below KBA;
https://me.sap.com/notes/0002628604 

But, Is it same for MANUAL Document Generation ? Does Event Reasons/Actions using business rules supports Manual Document Generation?

Thanks in Advance.

 

Regards,

P Shubham

entry.title: Salary Advance (Eligibility for Advances) can we consider all the records in Job information?
entry.content:
In SF-EC,Is there any way for Salary Advance (Eligibility for Advances) we can consider all the records in Job information. Based on the business rule/ eligibility rule its only considering the top record.

Scenario is we need to enable salary advance for people who went on deputation. Its considering only if it a top record. If at all any action is passed on top of the deputation action, then that eligibility is not getting considered.

entry.title: Earned Leave accrual calculation based on Paid Days - Time Off
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
 
Requirement : Employees are entitled to 30 days of EL, given as monthly frequency 2.5 days. Monthly calculation is based on Paid days in a month which includes working days, holidays and week offs.
 
Calculation Logic : ELs = {(no. of paid days in a month x 2.5 ELs)/total no. of days in a month}
 
Scenario : If in May, the employee has 3 LWPs, 2.25 EL will be credited for that month, although if the employee then regularizes these LWP into a paid leave or missed punch, then in June month, he should get the deducted EL(0.25) from May month. Hence in June, he accrues 2.5+0.25=2.75 EL.
 
Please let me know, in case anyone has encountered a similar requirement.

entry.title: Can successfactors EMLA EC connect to Test Instance of Employee central Payroll?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have an Instance of Employee Central provided as a part of eMLA offering for the project. Can we use it to connect to Test Instance of Employee Central Payroll?

entry.title: Manage My Team Tile Error: "An application error occurred"
entry.content:
Hello everyone, 

one manager gets the following error message when he tries to access Manage My Team Tile:

"An application error occurred. Please try again later, or notify support if the issue keeps occurring."



The manager has his own team but he can't manage them through the Manage My Tam Tile

Do you have any ideas what can cause this error message?

Best regards
Lukasz

entry.title: Setting up Custom email templates for SuccessFactors Employee Central Time Sheet
entry.content:
I would like to check if we can set up custom email templates for SuccessFactors Employee Central Time Sheet.

We have different scenarios where we would like to set up custom Email templates but we could not find any tokens that can be used to refer EC Time Sheet data. Like Time Sheet Start Date, Time sheet End Date, Status of the Time Sheet, Time Sheet Duration etc.

Has some one prepared custom email templates with above Time Sheet data included in the template ?

entry.title: employee central core certification preparation
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Can anyone guide me about the certification exam of Employee central core like :-

1. What is the format of the exam ?

2.From where i can get the practice questions ?

3.How do i know that now i am fully prepared for the exam  ?

4.How to clear it in my first attempt and what should my next plan after this certification?

Thanks & Regards

Kartik Chijwani

entry.title: Timesheet Module Behavior on Attendance and Leave Collision
entry.content:
Hello Experts,


I’m seeking your guidance regarding a standard behavior observed in the Timesheet module, specifically related to entry blocking when a collision occurs on a single day.

Issue Summary:
When both attendance and leave type (PL, CL,SL, Out Door Duty) or any absences are recorded on the same day, the system flags a collision error. As a result, it blocks timesheet entries for the remaining days of that week. However, once the collision is resolved, the timesheet updates correctly for the entire week.

We understand that this is standard system behavior.

Request for Suggestions:
Is there a way to prevent the system from blocking the remaining days of the week when such a conflict occurs on just one day?

If anyone has implemented a workaround or alternative solution to handle this scenario more gracefully, we would greatly appreciate your insights or suggestions.

entry.title: Offcycle event batch to "termination details".
entry.content: In SuccessFactors we have offcycle event batch for job information and employment details section. Can't that be added to termination details as well? Because to the standard field "Ok To Rehire" I think it should not always be a manual data change. For example in our client side we have a rule saying that a user cannot be "ok to rehire = NO" for more than 20 years. So they suggested us to make make an automation based on a custom date field where they decide when the employee should be ok to be rehired again. It's obviously going to be a future dated field and there is no rules to make it happen. So off cycle event batch could have been a good option if that was feasible. Currently we might have to do it manually.

entry.title: DRTM - Data Retention Management & Purge for Inactive users
entry.content: Documentation

entry.title: Limitation on Quantity 1-5 fields on Accrual Calculation Account Type Base Object
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

Can we define custom fields like quantity6, quantity7 in following 2 objects under Configure Object Definition? 

  1. Accrual Calculation Account Type Base
  2. Accrual Calculation Base

As of now, we do have actual quantity, quantity 1 to 5 standard SF fields. These fields are used to generate accruals from recorded timesheet. We are almost using all these fields. We would like to check if we can define custom fields after quantity5 and use those fields to generate accrual from timesheet via Integration Centre. 

Would like to check if SAP SuccessFactors product support custom fields in Accrual Calculation Account Type Base Object. 

Thanks & Regards,
Arijit

Note - We do not have time tracking license.

entry.title: Auto Person ID generation for Hire/Rehire
entry.content:
Hi,

I created an auto person ID generation rule and assigned it to the personInfo HRIS element in Manage Business Configuration (MBC), but the rule is not triggering.
